spec.goal: Implement the first runtime slice of post-In-Review autonomy by letting Maestro classify an owned PR-backed lane as wait_for_review, needs_review_repair, ready_to_land, or blocked from authoritative GitHub review and check state, while making service-config secret authority explicit through TOML-only `*_env_var` fields.
success_criteria: Maestro can re-read the recorded PR for an owned In Review lane and classify the lane as wait_for_review, needs_review_repair, ready_to_land, or blocked without mutating the lane.
The classification uses authoritative PR identity, review-thread state, review verdicts, required-check state, and mergeability instead of user chat relay.
The current runtime exposes the classification through status or deterministic logs for the owned lane and handles the absence of an eligible In Review lane explicitly.
Docs and targeted tests cover the classification rules and the ownership boundary between signal intake and later repair or landing work.

Service config accepts only explicit TOML-declared secret env-var names such as `tracker.api_key_env_var` and `github.token_env_var`; it does not accept literal tokens, `$ENV_VAR` value overloading, or ambient GitHub auth fallback.
constraints: Implement against the accepted XY-160 and XY-176 lifecycle and owned-lane policy instead of inventing new skill-specific states.
Do not perform repair, merge, closeout, or cleanup in this issue.
Do not rely on Linear comment rereads alone for PR identity because the current tracker abstraction does not expose comment or attachment history.
Keep the branch rooted at the latest origin/main in the isolated XY-173 workspace; do not touch the root checkout's local WORKFLOW.md modification.
Do not preserve compatibility with the old `tracker.api_key`, `github.token`, `$ENV_VAR` value syntax, or ambient `gh auth login` fallback; this batch should fail fast on the new explicit schema.

defaults.status_surface: reuse local status/reporting surfaces before adding new commands
defaults.authoritative_pr_identity: must be persisted by the runtime, not reconstructed from chat
defaults.secret_authority: service config names the required environment variables explicitly via `*_env_var`; runtime does not infer auth from ambient shell state

tasks.title: Pin the persisted review-handoff identity and signal model
tasks.objective: Trace where an owned In Review lane can recover its authoritative PR identity today, extend persisted runtime state where needed, and codify the exact signal inputs XY-173 will classify.
outputs: Persisted runtime representation for the owned review handoff PR identity and lineage
Documented classifier input set bound to existing retained-lane state
verification: Targeted unit tests for persisted review-handoff identity or recovery metadata

tasks.title: Implement the review-state classifier and operator-visible surface
tasks.objective: Add the GitHub review/check classifier for owned In Review lanes, wire it into the runtime's local status/reporting surface, and make the no-lane path deterministic.
outputs: Classifier implementation returning wait_for_review, needs_review_repair, ready_to_land, or blocked
Status/log output exposing the classification for the owned lane
Tests covering wait, repair, ready_to_land, blocked, and no-lane cases

tasks.title: Align docs and finish verification for the slice
tasks.objective: Update specs and plan evidence so the accepted runtime surface, operator expectations, and tests match the implemented classifier slice.
outputs: Spec and doc updates for the review-state classifier slice
Plan evidence recording the implementation and verification outcomes

tasks.title: Replace dual-use secret fields with explicit env-var contract
tasks.objective: Change the service config schema, runtime resolution, and GitHub auth plumbing to use TOML-only `tracker.api_key_env_var` and `github.token_env_var` fields with no compatibility fallback.
inputs: User directive to remove compatibility and ambient auth fallback
outputs: Updated config schema and auth resolution code
Regression tests rejecting the old fields and fallback behavior
verification: Targeted config and GitHub auth tests

tasks.title: Align docs evidence and finish verification for the env-var contract
tasks.objective: Update examples, runbooks, and plan evidence so the new explicit `*_env_var` contract is the only documented path, then rerun the full repo gates.
inputs: Current pilot and example config docs
outputs: Plan evidence for the contract change and full verification

triggers: The retained-lane state cannot safely persist PR identity without expanding the tracker abstraction or state model beyond the scoped classifier slice.
The smallest safe operator-visible surface is not the existing status/log path and would require a new command or wire protocol.
Authoritative GitHub review or check signals differ materially from the XY-160 lifecycle assumptions.

evidence: Added `src/github.rs` for repo-local GitHub identity routing plus PR URL parsing, and persisted `.maestro-review-handoff` markers in `src/state.rs` and `src/agent/tracker_tool_bridge.rs` so retained review lanes can rebind to authoritative PR identity without relying on tracker comment rereads.

Implemented post-review lane classification in `src/orchestrator.rs` using GitHub GraphQL review/check state and exposed it through the existing operator `status` surface as `wait_for_review`, `needs_review_repair`, `ready_to_land`, or `blocked`.

Added regression coverage for review handoff marker roundtrip, review handoff marker persistence, ready-to-land status, missing-marker blocked status, unresolved-thread repair classification, and repo-local GitHub identity routing.

Updated `docs/spec/system_maestro_runtime.md` and `docs/spec/system_maestro_post_review_lifecycle.md` to document the `.maestro-review-handoff` marker and the read-only post-review status surface introduced by XY-173.
Verification passed on the XY-173 lane: `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, and `git diff --check`.

Pre-commit self-review tightened the status surface so a GitHub PR state read failure degrades the affected post-review lane to `blocked` with reason `pull_request_state_read_failed` instead of aborting the entire `status` command.

Review repair propagated `codex.github-identity` and `codex.linear-workspace` into clone-backed workspaces so lane-local GitHub and Linear routing survives `issue_review_handoff` and post-review status inspection without manual workspace config.

Review repair paginated GitHub review-thread inspection in `src/orchestrator.rs`, counted unresolved threads beyond the first page, and added guards plus tests for mixed-snapshot pagination and missing end-cursor responses.

Late review repair now refreshes managed `.workspaces/*` lanes before post-review GitHub inspection, downgrades refresh failures to a lane-local `workspace_git_metadata_refresh_failed` block, and treats `reviewDecision = null` as approval-satisfied when checks are green and the PR is mergeable so zero-required-review repositories can reach `ready_to_land`.

Fresh verification for the late review repair passed: `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_ready_to_land_allows_zero_required_review_repos -- --nocapture`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check`.

Review repair replaced the hard-coded `codex.github-identity -> GITHUB_PAT_X/Y` mapping with optional `github.token` service-config resolution plus ambient `GH_TOKEN` or `gh auth login` fallback, threaded the configured token through review handoff validation, and removed the post-review status path's retained-workspace refresh so `maestro status` stays read-only for owned lanes. Fresh verification passed: `cargo test review_handoff_inspection_uses_configured_github_token -- --nocapture`, `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_leaves_managed_workspace_git_metadata_untouched -- --nocapture`, `cargo test resolves_optional_github_token_reference -- --nocapture`, `cargo test rejects_empty_github_token_when_present -- --nocapture`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, and `cargo make test`.

Switched the service-config secret contract to explicit TOML-declared env-var names only: `tracker.api_key_env_var` and `github.token_env_var`. The runtime now rejects legacy `tracker.api_key`, `github.token`, and `$ENV_VAR` value overloading, and GitHub operations require configured token authority instead of ambient `GH_TOKEN` or `gh auth login` fallback. Updated `maestro.example.toml`, `README.md`, and `docs/guide/pilot.md`, then reran targeted auth/config tests plus full verification: `cargo test configure_gh_command --manifest-path .workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test resolve_required_github_token_rejects_missing_token_env_var --manifest-path .workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test review_handoff_inspection_uses_configured_github_token --manifest-path .workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_ --manifest-path .workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test rejects_legacy_tracker_api_key_field --manifest-path .workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check`.

Final pre-push self-review removed the last optional GitHub token resolver helper so the checked-in service-config API exposes only the explicit required-token path for GitHub-backed flows, then reran `cargo test resolves_required_github_token_from_env_var_name --manifest-path .workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check` on the final diff.

Review repair on PR #21 deferred GitHub token resolution to lane-local operations, so `maestro status` and normal issue runs no longer fail eagerly when `github.token_env_var` is absent or unreadable. `build_post_review_lane_statuses()` now downgrades retained workspace handoff-record or local-head read failures to lane-local `blocked` results instead of aborting the whole status surface, and PR-backed handoff validation resolves `github.token_env_var` only when `issue_review_handoff` actually inspects GitHub. Fresh verification passed: `cargo test review_handoff_inspection_requires_configured_github_token_env_var --manifest-path /Users/xavier/code/trusted/y/hack-ink/maestro/.workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_blocks_workspace_head_read_failures --manifest-path /Users/xavier/code/trusted/y/hack-ink/maestro/.workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_blocks_missing_github_token_env_var --manifest-path /Users/xavier/code/trusted/y/hack-ink/maestro/.workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check`.

Latest review repair on PR #21 now fails live runs before agent execution only on dispatch paths that can actually reach GitHub-backed review handoff, keeps empty-backlog and post-refresh ineligible paths green, and classifies optional failing checks from `mergeStateStatus` instead of treating every failed rollup entry as required-check failure. Updated the `prepare_issue_run_*` live-success fixtures to use explicit GitHub token authority and reran `cargo test prepare_issue_run_records_starting_attempt_before_execute --manifest-path /Users/xavier/code/trusted/y/hack-ink/maestro/.workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test prepare_issue_run_uses_persisted_retry_budget_marker_after_restart --manifest-path /Users/xavier/code/trusted/y/hack-ink/maestro/.workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test prepare_issue_run_keeps_persisted_retry_budget_when_preferred_base_is_stale --manifest-path /Users/xavier/code/trusted/y/hack-ink/maestro/.workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test prepare_issue_run_honors_preferred_identity_when_attempt_is_current --manifest-path /Users/xavier/code/trusted/y/hack-ink/maestro/.workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo test prepare_issue_run_clears_terminal_guard_marker_when_new_attempt_starts --manifest-path /Users/xavier/code/trusted/y/hack-ink/maestro/.workspaces/XY-173/Cargo.toml`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check`.

Review repair now preserves reused retained workspaces when `github.token_env_var` preflight fails in `prepare_issue_run()`, while still cleaning newly created workspaces from the failed live attempt. Added regressions `live_run_with_candidate_requires_github_token_authority_before_agent_execution` and `live_run_preserves_reused_workspace_when_github_token_preflight_fails`, then reran three local self-review passes plus fresh verification: `cargo test live_run_with_candidate_requires_github_token_authority_before_agent_execution -- --nocapture`, `cargo test live_run_preserves_reused_workspace_when_github_token_preflight_fails -- --nocapture`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check`.

Late review repair on PR #21 now blocks post-review lanes only for explicit `maestro:manual-only` or `maestro:needs-attention` labels instead of reusing the broader pre-review nonactive gate, and it classifies `REVIEW_REQUIRED` lanes with required-check failures as `needs_review_repair` before falling back to `wait_for_review`. Fresh verification passed: `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_reports_ready_to_land -- --nocapture`, `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_leaves_managed_workspace_git_metadata_untouched -- --nocapture`, `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_blocks_missing_review_handoff_record -- --nocapture`, `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_blocks_workspace_head_read_failures -- --nocapture`, `cargo test build_post_review_lane_statuses_blocks_nonactive_labeled_post_review_issues -- --nocapture`, `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_requires_review_repair_before_review_when_required_checks_fail -- --nocapture`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check`.

Final late-review repair on PR #21 tightened GitHub token authority and PR review-state handling: blank `github.token_env_var` values now fail fast in live preflight, post-review status inspection, and PR-backed review handoff validation; GitHub PR snapshots now carry `reviewRequests.totalCount`, so `reviewDecision = null` remains `wait_for_review` while any review request is pending and only zero-request repos can treat `null` as approval-satisfied. Before pushing this repair, I ran three local self-review passes covering diff shape, contract consistency, and failure-surface coverage, then reran `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_waits_when_review_decision_is_null_but_review_request_is_pending -- --nocapture`, `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_blocks_blank_github_token_env_var -- --nocapture`, `cargo test merge_pull_request_review_state_page_rejects_changed_pending_review_request_count -- --nocapture`, `cargo test validate_review_handoff_runtime_rejects_blank_github_token_env_var -- --nocapture`, `cargo test review_handoff_inspection_rejects_blank_github_token_env_var -- --nocapture`, `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_ready_to_land_allows_zero_required_review_repos -- --nocapture`, `cargo test live_run_with_candidate_requires_github_token_authority_before_agent_execution -- --nocapture`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check`.

Newest review repair on PR #21 closes the remaining classifier ordering gap: conflicted pull requests now surface as `pull_request_not_mergeable` before either `REVIEW_REQUIRED` or pending review-request waits can return `wait_for_review`. I ran three local self-review passes before staging this batch, then reran `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_blocks_conflicted_pull_request_before_review_required_wait -- --nocapture`, `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_blocks_conflicted_pull_request_before_pending_review_wait -- --nocapture`, `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_requires_review_repair_before_review_when_required_checks_fail -- --nocapture`, `cargo test classify_post_review_lane_waits_when_review_decision_is_null_but_review_request_is_pending -- --nocapture`, `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, plan validate/read-back, and `git diff --check`.

key_findings: The tracker abstraction currently exposes issue fields, labels, blockers, and write-side comments, but not comment or attachment rereads.
The accepted XY-160 lifecycle already defines the classifier outcomes and authoritative signals for post-In-Review lanes.
The new XY-173 lane had to be rebased to origin/main because the root checkout is intentionally behind and carries a local WORKFLOW.md modification.
